danger_signals.visual_form: Red/hostile ship markers on sensors manager display; visual designs distinctly different from player faction
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring defensive action or retreat

danger_signals.visual_form: Fire and smoke visual effects on mothership model; health bar depletion; active beam/missile impact animations
danger_signals.meaning: Critical mission failure imminent; player base is vulnerable

danger_signals.visual_form: Progressive fire and smoke effects added to ship models as health depletes; visual degradation of hull integrity
danger_signals.meaning: Entity approaching destruction; imminent loss if not repaired or withdrawn

danger_signals.visual_form: Limited visible stationary rocks, gas clouds, or dust clouds in level sphere; harvester ships unable to locate mining sites
danger_signals.meaning: Economic constraint; fleet expansion limited; vulnerability to prolonged engagement

safety_signals.visual_form: Resource collector ships actively extracting from visible clouds/rocks; material transfer animations to mothership
safety_signals.meaning: Economic stability; fleet growth capacity maintained

safety_signals.visual_form: Absence of fire/smoke effects on mothership; full health bar; stationary, operational stance
safety_signals.meaning: Base secure; production capability intact; mission continuation viable

safety_signals.visual_form: Ships organized in tactical formations (wedges, spheres); grouped visual clustering in 3D space
safety_signals.meaning: Combat readiness; tactical advantage; coordinated defensive/offensive posture

safety_signals.visual_form: Narrative cutscenes between levels; objective markers fulfilled in level progression
safety_signals.meaning: Strategic progress; pathway to homeworld reclamation advancing

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinctive ship hull designs specific to Kushan fleet configuration
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Taiidan Empire ships have visually distinct design language; mothership and escort vessels use alternative aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mothership serves as faction base; ship construction queue reflects Kushan technological lineage; salvaged enemy vessels visually contrast with native fleet

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Visually distinct warship designs; empire vessels display different hull profiles from Kushan alternatives
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player can select Taiidan fleet control at campaign start, changing all ship designs while maintaining gameplay mechanics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy fleet composition in levels reflects Taiidan military doctrine; encountered vessels appear throughout campaign as primary hostile force

faction_indicators.faction: Pirates, mercenaries, traders, and rebels (Encountered factions)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct from both Kushan and Taiidan; specialized ship types indicate faction role (trader vs. combatant)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear throughout campaign journey; visual differentiation marks them as neutral or conditional threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Campaign narrative frames destruction through hand-drawn, black-and-white cutscenes with voiceovers; mothership presented as sole surviving mobile base
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mothership unable to move during single-player campaign; stationary defensive posture reflects refugee/survivor status; dependence on fleet for protection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy vessels present in level spheres; attack patterns against mothership and fleet; beam and missile weapon impact visual effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous combat engagement required; enemy fleet composition varies by mission; must defend mothership while pursuing objectives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High density of stationary rocks, gas clouds, and dust clouds visible in level environment; accessible harvesting sites distributed through 3D space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Resource collectors actively harvest; mothership constructs new ships at accelerated pace; economic expansion cycle enabled

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hyperspace jump sequences initiate level transitions; fighters and corvettes dock with mothership; larger ships line up for departure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multi-level campaign progression; fleet carries over between levels; cumulative ship acquisition demonstrates journey advancement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Novel ship types appear in buildable options; captured enemy vessels introduce alternative designs; research vessel facilitates visual fleet diversification
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Expanded combat and support capabilities; new specialized functions (repair corvettes, non-combat research vessels); tactical flexibility increases

environmental_storytelling.example: Black-and-white animated sequences between 16 campaign levels with voiceover narration establish plot developments, faction motivations, and story context without in-engine cinematics
Kushan vs. Taiidan fleet aesthetics differentiate factions through hull profiles, engine configurations, and weapon placements; visual language communicates technological heritage and empire identity

In-game screenshot depicts mothership under active attack with visible fire and smoke; stationary position and defensive role communicate survivor/base status; inability to relocate establishes narrative stakes
Level design varies cloud/rock density; environmental mining availability directly reflects regional resource conditions and mission urgency
Ship persistence across levels; visual fleet growth demonstrates journey progression toward homeworld reclamation; salvaged enemy vessels represent territorial gains

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary; constructs new ships; accepts harvested resources; provides docking points for fighter/corvette refueling
Displays fire and smoke visual effects; health bar depletes; becomes mission failure condition if destroyed; cannot move to escape
Mines stationary clouds/rocks; transports material to mothership; indicates economic stability and fleet expansion capacity
Grouped in tactical formations (wedges, spheres); responsive to player commands; requires periodic docking for refuel; rapid mobility in 3D space
Returns to mothership or support vessel for docking; visual damage effects accumulate; combat effectiveness diminishes
Visible on sensors manager with distinct hostile markers; fires beam weapons and missiles; attempts mothership/fleet destruction
Health bar depletes; fire and smoke effects accumulate; becomes target for salvage corvette capture; visual degradation signals vulnerability
Tows damaged enemy ships to mothership; converts captured vessels into player fleet assets; visual tether shows active capture state

notes: Homeworld's manifestation layer relies heavily on 3D spatial visualization and persistent fleet state communication. Faction identity is primarily conveyed through ship design aesthetics rather than color coding, a significant departure from 2D RTS conventions. The stationary mothership creates narrative vulnerability and establishes geographic anchor for world-state perception. Resource scarcity and abundance are communicated through environmental density variation. Damage progression uses cumulative visual effects (fire/smoke) rather than discrete state changes. Hyperspace jump mechanics serve dual narrative/gameplay purpose: advancing story while making fleet composition visually explicit through docking animations.
